content: The working memory capacity limits that constrain agent reasoning can be framed as an implicit numerical planning problem, where a Numerical Planning Operator Learner can be trained to dynamically allocate limited working memory slots as discrete numerical resources across sequential reasoning steps. This creates a system that automatically prunes low-value working memory traces and reserves slots for high-impact reasoning steps, matching working memory supply to the numerical demands of the current reasoning task.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
